title: Handles for Communicating With COM Objects
breadcrumbs: Handles for Communicating With COM Objects
text: Handles for Communicating With COM Objects The handle data type in Uniface provides a mechanism to communicate with external objects via COM. Using an object's handle, Uniface can call-out to an external COM object, and an external COM object can call-in to Uniface. The handle data type is Uniface's equivalent of the IDispatch interface in COM, and holds a reference to an instantiated object. In the Signature Editor the underlying type of the handle data type is VT_Dispatch. The handle datatype can be passed to a function as an input or output parameter. Note: In previous versions of Uniface, an instantiated object was addressed using the String datatype, because no special data type had been defined. In this case, the String data type functioned as a Uniface version of the IDispatch interface in COM. Related Links COM and Uniface Parameter Data Types Converting Uniface parameter data types to and from the COM environment has both a static and dynamic nature. When you export a COM Interface DLL, the data types of the signature definitions must be matched to the appropriate COM types, taking into account the user’s implementation selections. At runtime, both Uniface and COM allow the user to pass variants for parameters, so the connector can dynamically convert data types, within reason. Handles A handle is a reference to an object, such as a synchronous component instance, entity, occurrence, or field. Handles can be used to call operations on the referenced object, or to pass these objects as variables and parameters. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
